justification: This large-scale survey directly studies AI programming assistants (Copilot, ChatGPT, Tabnine), covering prompting, control, feedback, evaluation, and non-functional requirements which are highly relevant to designing agentic systems that write code for data analysis. It is not specific to scientific or data-science tasks, but its usability findings and recommended interaction designs are applicable to agentic assistants for data and natural scientists.

The article describes Bio-Copilot, a multi-agent LLM-driven system that plans, writes, executes, and debugs analysis code for large-scale omics workflows and supports human–agent collaboration, directly matching an agentic AI for scientific/data-analysis code generation.

This paper addresses human-AI collaborative decision systems for retail (reinforcement learning, fuzzy logic, XAI, bias detection) rather than agentic AI that generates or assists in writing data-analysis source code for data scientists or natural scientists, so it is only loosely related to the target system.
